title: Vlcc Second Hand Prices At Decade Highs
section_path: Vlcc Second Hand Prices At Decade Highs
section_path_text: Vlcc Second Hand Prices At Decade Highs
summary:
Main
● VLCC asset prices at decade highs: 5Y units >$120m (+17% YoY); 10Y units >$100m (+20% YoY). ● Dirty freight at record levels: TD3C >$200k/day (+440% YoY); BDTI at new all-time high. ● Clean market strengthening: MR USG-Continent +530% YoY; LR2 and LR1 routes sharply higher (+100% and +80% YoY, respectively).

children.title: Main
children.summary: ● VLCC asset prices at decade highs: 5Y units >$120m (+17% YoY); 10Y units >$100m (+20% YoY). ● Dirty freight at record levels: TD3C >$200k/day (+440% YoY); BDTI at new all-time high.

children.title: Spotlight of the Week | VLCC Second-hand Market
section_path: Spotlight of the Week | VLCC Second-hand Market
children.section_path_text: Spotlight of the Week | VLCC Second-hand Market
children.summary:
Secondhand Values Repriced
The improvement in one-year time charter earnings has reshaped the VLCC secondhand market. The recent strong cash generation and improved freight visibility have pushed asset values sharply higher, not only for modern units but also for overaged units.

children.title: Freight Market Overview - Dirty
children.summary: The Baltic Dirty Tanker Index has reached a new historical high, exceeding 1,900 points (+120% YoY).

children.title: VLCC | Suezmax | Aframax ↑
children.summary: Before the month's end, the freight market has reached elevated levels. Specifically, the Time Charter Equivalent (TCE) for VLCCs on the Middle East Gulf-to-China route has surged past the $200,000/day threshold, marking a substantial year-over-year increase of over 440%.

children.title: Freight Market Overview - Clean
children.summary: Baltic Clean Tanker Index (+23% YoY). Although the index has stayed below 900 points and weakened since early February, it rose by nearly 7% week-on-week.

children.title: LR2 | LR1 | MR ↑
children.summary:
TC1 75k Middle East Gulf to Japan | TC5 55k Middle East Gulf to Japan | TC14 38k US Gulf to Japan
The clean segment is concluding the final week of February with a clear upward momentum. The MR USG to Continent route remains the top performer in terms of year-on-year growth, as highlighted in our previous Tanker Market Monitor .

children.title: Supply Market Trends
children.summary: Previous weekly market monitors concentrated on routes where our Signal Ocean Platform signals indicated an oversupplied market. This week, however, we shift our focus from oversupply to routes showing an undersupplied picture.

children.title: VLCC | AG & WAfr
children.summary: The latest Signal Ocean Platform figures indicate an undersupplied market on the TD3 route (-27% WoW ~80 Vessels). AG: The vessel count has fallen sharply to one of the lowest levels recorded in the first two months of the year, accelerating its decline since the end of January.

children.title: Suezmax | Wafr
children.summary: West Africa: Spot/relet vessels on the TD20 route reversed the upward trend recorded in our latest Tanker Market Monitor , and the current Signal Ocean Platform data indicate that the TD20 route is now undersupplied (-30% vs the 3-month average), with a 29% weekly increase in freight rates.

children.title: VLCC ↓ 1.9% WoW | Suezmax ↓ 4.8% WoW | Aframax ↓ 7.8% WoW
section_path: VLCC ↓ 1.9% WoW | Suezmax ↓ 4.8% WoW | Aframax ↓ 7.8% WoW
children.section_path_text: VLCC ↓ 1.9% WoW | Suezmax ↓ 4.8% WoW | Aframax ↓ 7.8% WoW
children.summary: The tonne-mile indices for all dirty tanker segments softened during the week. VLCCs continued to trail the other segments, with its index dropping 1.9 percentage points (ppts) to 96.3% from 98.2%, remaining below the 100% threshold.

children.title: Aframax ↑ 4.1% WoW | Panamax ↓ 3.2% WoW | MR2 ↓ 10.4% WoW
section_path: Aframax ↑ 4.1% WoW | Panamax ↓ 3.2% WoW | MR2 ↓ 10.4% WoW
children.section_path_text: Aframax ↑ 4.1% WoW | Panamax ↓ 3.2% WoW | MR2 ↓ 10.4% WoW
children.summary: The MR segment, while still the strongest performer overall, experienced a significant correction, falling 12.0 percentage points from 115.8% to 103.8%. The Aframax segment saw an increase in its tonne-mile index, rising by 3.5 percentage points from 85.3% to 88.8%.
